name: bubble_sort_grounded
topic: Python bubble sort ascending and descending
retrieval_context: Bubble sort repeatedly compares adjacent elements and swaps them when they are out of order. After each outer pass, the largest remaining value is placed at the end for ascending order, so the inner loop can stop at n - 1 - i.
A descending bubble sort can reverse the comparison and swap adjacent values when the left value is smaller than the right value.
narration: Bubble sort compares adjacent elements, swaps them when needed, and shrinks the inner-loop bound because each pass places one extreme value at the end.

name: list_comprehension_grounded
topic: Python list comprehensions for even squares
retrieval_context: A list comprehension creates a new list in one expression using square brackets. It can include an expression, a for clause, and an optional if filter.
The expression x ** 2 squares each selected value. The filter x % 2 == 0 keeps even numbers.
code:
numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]
squares = [x ** 2 for x in numbers if x % 2 == 0]
print(squares)
narration: The list comprehension builds a new list inside square brackets, squares x, loops over numbers, and keeps only even values.

name: range_loop_grounded
retrieval_context: range(10) produces the integer sequence from 0 up to but not including 10. A for loop can iterate over that sequence one value at a time.
The loop variable receives each value from the range in order.
narration: The range call produces values from zero up to but not including ten, and the for loop prints each value.
